Here's a curated list of the highest-value features to set up for your Windows 11 + game dev workflow:

---

## Top Features to Configure

### Immediate wins (env vars)
| Feature | Env Var | Why |
|---------|---------|-----|
| Flicker-free rendering | `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` | Cleaner terminal output on Windows |
| PowerShell tool | `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_POWERSHELL_TOOL=1` | Native Windows shell support |
| Prompt caching (1hr) | `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1` | Faster, cheaper responses in long sessions |
| Subprocess credential scrub | `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB=1` | Security hygiene |

### Settings.json additions
| Feature | Config | Why |
|---------|--------|-----|
| Auto mode | `"defaultMode": "auto"` | Skip permission prompts for safe actions |
| Thinking summaries | `"showThinkingSummaries": true` | See Claude's reasoning |

### New hooks worth adding
- **`PreCompact`** â€” run a git commit before context compaction so nothing is lost
- **`CwdChanged`** â€” auto-activate project env when switching dirs
- **`PermissionDenied`** with `{retry: true}` â€” auto-retry in auto mode

### New slash commands to know
- `/ultrareview` â€” deep multi-agent code review
- `/effort` â€” interactive effort level slider (for complex game work)
- `/less-permission-prompts` â€” auto-generate your allowlist (you already have this skill)
- `/loop` â€” run a prompt on a recurring interval

---

Want me to set up all of these at once, or pick specific ones? I'd suggest starting with **env vars + auto mode + PreCompact hook** as the highest-impact combo.

Both files are valid JSON. Here's what was configured:

**User settings** (`~/.claude/settings.json`):
- `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` â€” flicker-free alt-screen rendering
- `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_POWERSHELL_TOOL=1` â€” native Windows PowerShell tool
- `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1` â€” 1-hour prompt cache TTL
- `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB=1` â€” strip credentials from subprocesses
- `permissions.defaultMode: "auto"` â€” skip prompts for safe actions
- `showThinkingSummaries: true` â€” see Claude's reasoning in transcript

**Project settings** (`.claude/settings.json`) â€” added alongside existing hooks:
- **PreCompact**: git checkpoint before compaction (`git add -A && git commit -m "pre-compact checkpoint"`)
- **CwdChanged**: auto-runs `direnv allow` if `.envrc` exists in the new dir
- **PermissionDenied**: outputs `{"retry": true}` so auto mode re-evaluates denied actions

Restart Claude Code for the env vars to take effect.

These MCP servers use the built-in claude.ai connector flow. To authenticate all three:

1. Type `/mcp` in the prompt
2. Select **"claude.ai Gmail"** â†’ follow the OAuth link
3. Type `/mcp` again â†’ select **"claude.ai Google Calendar"** â†’ follow the OAuth link
4. Type `/mcp` again â†’ select **"claude.ai Google Drive"** â†’ follow the OAuth link

Each one will open a Google OAuth page in your browser â€” sign in with `noahmbrown2@gmail.com` and grant access. Once done, the full Gmail/Calendar/Drive tools will be available in this session.
The file exists. What would you like to do with `bitlife-finance.html`?

Everything is wired up correctly. The browser-sync server should have auto-reloaded. Here's what was added:

**Inbox Column** (260px, right of Education column, hides below 960px):
- Shows newest newsletter first with unread dot indicator (â—�) and gold border
- Red badge showing unread count in the header
- Preview snippet of the president headline

**Monthly Newsletter** (generated every Age +1 click) containing:
- ðŸ—³ï¸� **White House Update** â€” 5 fictional presidents cycle every 4 game years (Holt â†’ Reeves â†’ Caldwell â†’ Chen â†’ Thornton), each with their own policy flavor and 6 rotating action headlines
- âš”ï¸� **Global Conflicts** â€” 8 fictional wars (Caspian oil dispute, Pacific trade war, Gulfara insurgency, etc.) that randomly start, escalate, and resolve; directly reference gas/inflation effects
- â›½ **Gas & Inflation** â€” 5 different descriptions tied to current market climate (recession/bear/normal/bull/bubble)
- ðŸ“œ **Laws Passed** â€” 1â€“2 random laws from a pool of 18, each with economic consequence notes

Done. Here's what changed:

- **Start with a job** â€” on game start, a random job matching your education level is auto-assigned (broke gets high-school jobs, middle/rich get degree-level jobs)
- **Annual career window** â€” every year when you age, a "Annual Career Opportunity" popup appears showing up to 4 higher-paying jobs. Each shows `[X% success]` where X is exactly your current Smarts score
- **Smarts = success %** â€” if Smarts is 74, you have a 74% shot at landing each job. Fail and you get rejected; pass and you start at that job's salary range. You can also "Stay" to skip
- **Find Job button** â€” now blocked if you're employed (redirects you to wait for the annual window); still works if you get laid off so you can recover manually

All functions are present. The browser-sync server auto-reloaded. Here's what clicking Stocks or Crypto now shows:

**Stock/Crypto grid modal:**
- 2-column card grid â€” one card per stock (10 stocks) or crypto (4 coins)
- Each card shows:
  - Name + ticker
  - **All-time % change** (green/red) since game start â€” the full history chart
  - Current price + last-year % change
  - **Sparkline chart** â€” green line if up from start, red if down, with gradient fill and start/end dots
  - If owned: shows shares/coins held + current value + avg cost
  - **Buy** button (10 shares / $1,000 crypto) and **Sell All** button (grayed out if not owned)
- Buying/selling refreshes the modal in-place so charts stay visible
- Scrollable so all assets fit

The chart tracks `history[]` which records one price per year â€” so after 10 years you get a 10-point lifetime performance chart per asset.

Done. Refreshing the page now restores your character, finances, investments, newsletters, active wars, and full net worth history automatically. The new game screen only shows if there's no saved data.

The career window was being pushed to `GS.pendingEvents` inside the `isNewYear` block, then `GS.pendingEvents=[]` wiped it 35 lines later before it could ever show. Moved the push to **after** the reset so it's the first event queued each new year â€” career window now shows every 12 months as intended.

Done. Career window now triggers every 12 months at your current job instead of every calendar year. The `monthsAtJob` counter increments each month while employed and resets to 0 when you change jobs or become unemployed â€” so if you take a new job, the clock restarts and you wait another 12 months before the next window.

Now click "Age +1 Month" and if there's a hidden JS error it'll show as a toast. The two most likely causes:

1. **`GS.char` is null** â€” auto-load failed silently but the game screen is showing. The toast will say "No character loaded."
2. **A JS exception** somewhere inside `monthlyTick` â€” the toast will show the error message.

Try clicking the button and tell me what (if anything) shows up as a toast or if the age now increments visually.
Done â€” `bitlife-finance.html` is restored to the last committed state. All my changes (monthsAtJob counter, career window trigger, error handling) are gone.
Game is open at `http://localhost:3000/bitlife-finance.html` with live reload active.

Now the button will re-enable even if an error occurs, and you'll see a toast showing the error message. Click "Age +1 Month" again â€” the button should no longer get stuck, and tell me what the toast says (if any) so I can fix the underlying bug.
Opened `http://localhost:3000/bitlife-finance.html`. Click "Age +1 Month" and tell me what the red error toast says at the bottom of the screen.

12-month lock is verified working. Refresh your browser and start a new game â€” you'll now be locked into your starting job for 12 months, then get the career window every year.

**Top realism gaps the audit found (quick-hit priorities):**

1. **No rent** â€” biggest miss. 22-yos aren't paying $300 to live.
2. **Student loans lumped into generic debt** at 5% â€” real federal loans are ~6.5% with 10-yr fixed payment.
3. **Middle scenario only has $8k debt** â€” average grad carries ~$30k.
4. **Bachelor's salary cap is $110k** â€” way too high for entry-level; should be $75k max.
5. **No health insurance** premium/deductible line.
6. **No 401k employer match** (typical 3-4%).
7. **Job-hopping doesn't guarantee a raise** â€” real hops are 10-20% bumps.
8. **No standard deduction** in tax calc.
9. **Missing early-20s events** â€” first apartment, roommate drama, credit card offers, grad school decision.
10. **Credit score** ignores utilization and inquiries.

Want me to tackle these? I'd suggest starting with #1 (rent) + #3/4 (realistic debt/salary at char creation) + #9 (add 5-6 early-20s events) â€” those three shift the feel the most. Say the word and I'll implement.
Opened. If you want a fresh character (not the auto-loaded save), open DevTools console (F12) and run `localStorage.removeItem('finlife_save')` then refresh.

Done. Stocks and crypto now tick every month instead of yearly:

- **Stocks**: drift divided by 12 (monthly), volatility divided by âˆš12 (standard finance scaling) â€” so an 8% annual return still compounds to ~8%/yr, but you see the bumps every month.
- **Crypto**: same monthly scaling applied; big Â±30% swings can still happen but no longer all in one jump.
- **Market climate** (bull/bear/recession/bubble) still shifts annually â€” keeps regimes lasting years instead of flipping monthly.

Done. Click the **Stocks** or **Crypto** button in-game and you'll now see a 2-column grid with:

Refresh your browser and age a few months to see the history build up.

Done. Click **Real Estate** to manage housing:

- **Buy** a property â†’ if it's your first, you automatically move in (ðŸ�¡ tag)
- **Move into** any property you own â€” vacates your current home, cancels the rental if active
- **Move out of** the one you live in â†’ back to paying rent, frees the property to be rented out
- **Rent out** only appears for vacant properties (can't rent out the one you live in)
- **Sell** works regardless; if you sell the one you live in, `hasHouse` flips to false

**Financial impact:** Living in your own place replaces the ~$1,200/mo rent portion of expenses with your actual mortgage payment plus $300/mo upkeep. Properties you don't live in still generate rental income when rented.

**Mechanics:**

**Mechanics:**
- Each vehicle has a **monthly cost** (gas/electricity + insurance + maintenance) â€” EVs cost 40-50% less to run
- Values **depreciate monthly** (4-14% annual rate depending on type â€” EVs depreciate slower)
- Vehicle values count toward net worth
- Selling takes a 5% private-sale hit vs current market value
- Depreciation floors at 15% of original price (no vehicle goes fully to zero)

Your monthly cash flow will now also reflect all vehicle running costs â€” buy a jet and you'll pay $55k/mo. Refresh to see it.
